I think we're good. I'm going 5to try to move over to obsidian and continue work there
When I ask you to work... I'm getting lots of grayed out thinking info. I thought I turned that off in your config file?
Let's make them minimal
I want to make the global. Don't we need to update the config.tomal file?
So, we should edit AGENTS.md ?
That's fine. Let's update the AGENTS.md file
Thank you.

I would like to continue from ~/.codex/memories/csvdb_handoff.md however, it was generated on a SLURM cluster (called obsidian) where the large distance estimate improvement is currently running. I'd like to continue work on this machine so that we don't "trip over" the 30+ hour 640 core run that's occuring there.
I'm happy to have you install slurm code here so you can test things as desired
Not necessesary, I have that running in another window

Actually, there's going to be a large number of new codes added to the databse (ingest). While doing this, we should add the qdist distance calculations as we go. Can you write a plan so that all the analysis we've done so far occurs during the ingest?

I would like the capability of running slurm commands (e.g., squeue) from this machine. There is only one cluster (obsidian) and I've copied its slurm.conf to /tmp on this machine. Please update dotfiles to install the slurm client and to define the envvars that will allow me to do client commands to obsidian. Thanks!

The other thing we're going to change is instead of ingesting from my VM, we'll do it as a SLURM job on obsidian. This probably means we can use a lot more cores and distribute the work more intelligently. Please investigate how to leverage the cluster well for ingestion. The input files will still be coming from gcloud as before
If we're running on obsidian (via ssh)... we don't want to change that environment.
Might as well try to do all the stages. We can always roll it back if something isn't right. Just don't do any writing at this point to the BQ database.
You have permission to write to ~/.config/slurm
1. if fine. Please explain 2.
How much slower to run and will the benefit be noticable
Let's make it more serial... but keep a note on how to put it back if we decide it slows down enough to really notice. Most ingests will be big, so it's probably fine to do it.
Nice. Is there anything else we need to do to be ready for the next ingest?
Please put these instructions in the ~/.codex/memories/csvdb_
Please put these instructions in the ~/.codex/memories/csvdb_handoff.md
(I'll copy that over to the cluster when I continue you there)
I just tried sinfo in another window (after re-loading .zshrc)... and it just returned with no output
I can't find a way to make the slurm calls work under a `watch`
not important. I'll just use a shell while loop
This is fine. I just wanted something I could use remotely.
